free_text: Isotopic distributions of thermal-neutron-induced
fission fragments of near-symmetric fission of
239,241Pu determined using calorimetric low-temperature
detectors
The LOHENGRIN spectrometer is a parabola mass
spectrometer for separation of the ionized fission
fragments with respect to their mass over ionic charge
and velocity. For the nuclear charge identification
the passive absorber method with a novel detector
technology of calorimetric low temperature detectors
CLTDs measuring the residual energy after passage
through SiN absorber foils is applied.
4 um thick SiN degrader foil stack
The residual energy spectra of mass and energy
separated fragments from the LOHENGRIN spectrometer
are fitted with a sum of Gaussians to determine the
relative yields for the different nuclear charges.
Total uncertainties with contributions from
the following statistical and systematic
uncertainties:
- statistical uncertainty of the Gaussian fit.
uncertainty of the thermal neutron flux
instability
systematic uncertainty due to the
approximation used for fractional independent yield
calculations
Approved by S.Dubey 16 February 2022.
VS.
The 241Pu target had an effective area of 7 x 0.5 cm2,
was 24 ug/cm2 thick, and was covered by a 0.25 um
thick Ni foil.
